rows.query: 35 states reported expenditures for healthrelated r&d in fy 2016, yet 83% of all state government agency expenditures on health-related r&d was reported by agencies in five states: new york , texas , california , florida , and pennsylvania.
rows.code:
col = "health"
cali = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('california', case=False, na=False), col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
tx = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('texas', case=False, na=False), col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
ny = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('new york', case=False, na=False), col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
fl = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('florida', case=False, na=False), col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
pa = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('pennsylvania', case=False, na=False), col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
total = df[col].sum()
result = cali + tx + ny + fl + pa
print(result / total * 100)

rows.query: 52% of family class immigrants came from south asia, east asia and western developed countries.
rows.code:
family_class_col = "family class"
south_asia = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('percent > source region > southern asia'), family_class_col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
east_asia = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('percent > source region > east asia'), family_class_col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
western_developed = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('percent > source region > western developed countries'), family_class_col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
result = south_asia + east_asia + western_developed
print(result)

rows.query: after adjustment for inflation, federal s&e obligations to academic institutions declined by 7% from fy 2012 to fy 2013.

fy_2013 = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('current \$millions > 2013'), col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
decline = ((fy_2012 - fy_2013) / fy_2012) * 100
result = abs(decline - 7)
print(result)

rows.query: 1.5% the average pace of u.s. gross domestic product (gdp) over the 7-year period 2008-15.

revised = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['revised average growth of annual real gdp > percentage'].str.contains('2014|2015|2016'), 'revised average growth of annual real gdp > percentage'], errors='coerce').mean()

result = (previously_published + revised + mean_absolute_revision) / 3
print(result)

profound = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['audiometrically measured high-frequency hearing loss > number > \'000\''].notnull() & (df['unperceived hearing loss > percent'] == 64.6), 'audiometrically measured high-frequency hearing loss > number > \'000\''], errors='coerce').iloc[0]

rows.query: women were more likely than men to engage in less than 150 minutes per week of moderate-to-vigorous physical activity, have a family history of high blood pressure or early cardiovascular disease, or have ckd.
rows.code:
women_val = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('less than 150 minutes/week in moderate-to-vigorous physical activity', case=False, na=False), 'women > risk ratio'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
men_val = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('less than 150 minutes/week in moderate-to-vigorous physical activity', case=False, na=False), 'men > risk ratio'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]

family_high_bp_women = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('family history > high blood pressure', case=False, na=False), 'women > risk ratio'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
family_high_bp_men = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('family history > high blood pressure', case=False, na=False), 'men > risk ratio'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]

family_early_cv_women = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('family history > early cardiovascular disease', case=False, na=False), 'women > risk ratio'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
family_early_cv_men = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('family history > early cardiovascular disease', case=False, na=False), 'men > risk ratio'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]

ckd_women = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('chronic kidney disease', case=False, na=False), 'women > risk ratio'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
ckd_men = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('chronic kidney disease', case=False, na=False), 'men > risk ratio'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]

result = (women_val > men_val) and (family_high_bp_women > family_high_bp_men) and (family_early_cv_women > family_early_cv_men) and (ckd_women > ckd_men)
print(result)

rows.query: inuit in inuit nunangat were more likely than those outside, for example, those in rural areas , to gather for cultural reasons.
rows.code:
inuit_nunangat = pd.to_numeric(df.loc['row_14', 'reasons for gathering wild plants or berries in the previous 12 months > for cultural reasons'], errors='coerce')
rural = pd.to_numeric(df.loc['row_13', 'reasons for gathering wild plants or berries in the previous 12 months > for cultural reasons'], errors='coerce')
result = inuit_nunangat - rural
print(result)

rows.query: residents of the territories who had completed a university degree were more likely than those who had completed high school or less to believe that police were doing a good job enforcing the laws.
rows.code:
uni_degree = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['col[0]'].str.contains('university degree'), 'enforcing the laws'], errors='coerce').mean()
hs_or_less = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['col[0]'].str.contains('less than high school|high school diploma'), 'enforcing the laws'], errors='coerce').mean()
result = uni_degree - hs_or_less
print(result)

rows.query: yukon had a lower percentage of households that received formal home care compared to the rest of canada.
rows.code:
yukon = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('northwest territories', case=False, na=False), 'formal home care services > %'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
canada = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('total', case=False, na=False), 'formal home care services > %'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
result = canada - yukon
print(result)

rows.query: the highest representation of females accused was observed in cases of theft in 2015.

rows.query: during the 2000s the chronic low-income rate among all immigrants peaked at 16.3% in 2004 and declined to 12.3% by 2012.

result = vals[peak_year_2000s]
print(result)

rows.query: nova scotia had a significantly higher percentage of households that received formal home care services compared to the rest of canada in 2015/2016.
rows.code:
canada_total = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('total', case=False, na=False), 'formal home care services > %'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
nova_scotia = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('nova scotia', case=False, na=False), 'formal home care services > %'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
result = nova_scotia > canada_total
print(result)

rows.query: among the closest accused-victim relationship, males accused of homicide were most likely to kill an acquaintance from 2001 to 2015 in canada.
rows.code:
male_homicides = pd.to_numeric(df['male accused > number'], errors='coerce')
acquaintance_homicides = df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('acquaintance', case=False, na=False), 'male accused > number']
max_acquaintance_homicides = acquaintance_homicides.max()
result = male_homicides[male_homicides == max_acquaintance_homicides].index[0]
print(result)

rows.query: most of the total of u.s. r&d expenditures was experimental development at $388.6 billion, or 64%.
rows.code:
col = "2017a"
exp_dev_2017 = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('experimental development', case=False, na=False), col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
total_2017 = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('all r&d', case=False, na=False), col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
percentage = exp_dev_2017 / total_2017 * 100
result = f"{total_2017} or {percentage:.1f}%"
print(result)

rows.query: canadian households in large population centres were significantly less likely to have received formal home care than those in smaller population centres and rural areas in 2015/2016.
rows.code:
large_cities = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('large urban population centre'), 'formal home care services > %'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
small_centers = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('small population centre'), 'formal home care services > %'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
rural_areas = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('rural area'), 'formal home care services > %'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
result = (small_centers + rural_areas - 2 * large_cities) / 2
print(result)

rows.query: the three largest cmas, toronto which accounted for 48% of all robberies reported by police in 2015, had smaller changes.
rows.code:
toronto_robbery_rate = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['col'].str.contains('toronto'), 'robbery > rate'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
other_cmas_robbery_rate = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['col'].str.contains('homicide|sexual assault|breaking and entering|motor vehicle theft'), 'robbery > rate']).sum()
total_robbery_rate = toronto_robbery_rate + other_cmas_robbery_rate
percent_toronto = (toronto_robbery_rate / total_robbery_rate) * 100
result = percent_toronto >= 48
print(result)

rows.query: in 2016/2017, there were 5,937 youth that began a period of supervision in correctional services in the eight reporting jurisdictions, a 44% decrease from 2012/2013.
rows.code:
total_2016_2017 = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'] == '(total correctional services)', 'total-all jurisdictions > number'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
percent_change_2012_2013 = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'] == '(total correctional services)', 'total-all jurisdictions > percent change in the number from 2012/2013'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
result = total_2016_2017 / (1 + percent_change_2012_2013 / 100)
print(result)

rows.query: in 2018/2019, the rate of adults on probation decreased by 6% compared to 2017/2018.
rows.code:
probation_rate_2018 = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('probation', case=False, na=False), 'probate > rate'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
probation_rate_2017 = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('probation', case=False, na=False), 'probate > rate'].shift(1), errors='coerce').iloc[1]
percent_change = (probation_rate_2018 - probation_rate_2017) / probation_rate_2017 * 100
result = percent_change.round().iloc[0]
print(result)

rows.query: much of the increase in the rate of breaking and entering at the national level between 2014 and 2015 can be accounted for somewhat mitigated by a drop in the rate of break-ins in montreal.
rows.code:
montreal_breaking_entering_2015 = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['col'] == 'breaking and entering > rate', 'montreal'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
montreal_breaking_entering_change = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['col'] == 'breaking and entering > percent change in rate 2014 to 2015', 'montreal'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
montreal_contribution = montreal_breaking_entering_2015 * (montreal_breaking_entering_change / 100)
result = montreal_contribution
print(result)
